subsections.heading: 2.1 Background
content: Currently, vendor block/Unblock is handled through a mix of manual forms, email-based approvals, and direct entries into the ERP/procurement system, leading to inconsistent data quality, limited visibility, and longer cycle times. The absence of a standardised workflow and controls increases the risk of duplicate vendors, incomplete documentation, and non-compliance with internal and external regulations.

runs.text: Currently, vendor block/Unblock is handled through a mix of manual forms, email-based approvals, and direct entries into the ERP/procurement system, leading to inconsistent data quality, limited visibility, and longer cycle times. The absence of a standardised workflow and controls increases the risk of duplicate vendors, incomplete documentation, and non-compliance with internal and external regulations.

subsections.heading: 2.2 Objectives
content: The project aims to design and implement a controlled, workflow-driven vendor block /unblock process that enforces mandatory data fields, documentation checks, and role-based approvals. It also seeks to improve transparency and auditability through clear status tracking, standardized communications, and integration with the organization’s master data and finance systems.

It also seeks to improve transparency and auditability through clear status tracking, standardized communications, and integration with the organization’s master data and finance systems.

subsections.heading: 4.5.3 Approver Behaviour
content: Below are the common points for all the approvers.

Assignment to Approver(s): Once all the mandatory fields are entered, validated, and the request is submitted by the Initiator, the system automatically assigns the request to the appropriate approver based on the approver logic defined.
Approver will get an email notification. Similarly, approval and rejection criteria can also be defined, if there are a preset number of rejection reasons, those can be shown as a dropdown.
Approver access: Approvers have two methods to access the transaction, review and act on requests as mentioned below -
Email Notification:
The Manch system sends an email notification to the approver with a link to review the request.
Manch System Login:
The approver can log in to the Manch system, navigate to the "Pending Requests" section, and review the details.
Approver Behaviour: Based on all the information available to approver , they can approve or reject or send back.
If approver rejects, transaction will be in rejection status and no further actions are allowed in the request.
If approver send back, transaction will be assigned back to Initiator and the Initiator can view the comment and update the required data.
Once Initiator re-submits, transaction will follow all the steps of the workflow.
Below is the default email notification content that will be sent to the approver.

subsections.heading: 4.5.3.1 Approver Flow
content: Approver flow defines the sequence of roles that must review and approve a vendor block/ unblock request for India and Non-India entities.
Note: The Pidilite team to provide the approver derivation logic.
Approver flow – PIL and domestic subsidiaries
For India, the workflow will consist of the following approval levels:
Pending with Vertical Head – Review and approval by the relevant business/vertical head.

Pending with MDC Team – Final review by the MDC team. The MDC team can add ad hoc approvers as needed, up to a maximum of five parallel approvers. All ad hoc approvers must record their decisions, after which the request returns to the MDC approver, who then takes the final action in line with the defined common behaviour.
Approver flow – International Subsidiaries
For Non-India, the workflow will consist of four sequential approval levels:
Pending with Country Head – Review and approval by the respective country head.

Pending with MDC Team – Final review by the MDC team. The MDC team can add ad hoc approvers as needed, up to a maximum of five parallel approvers. All ad hoc approvers must record their decisions, after which the request returns to the MDC approver, who then takes the final action in line with the defined common behaviour.

Outbound: Data sent from the internal system to external systems.
Inbound: Data received from external systems into the internal system.

spoc.description: The process will be initiated by the initiator by logging into the system and clicking on the create option in the dashboard.
This process can be initiated by any user in the system. The user should be active in the Pidilite HRMS system. This data will be maintained in the Manch EDV system.

initiator.description: Login: The Pidilite team logs in to the Manch application using the provided credentials. The following basic details are shown to the user for each transaction created.
Company Name – Title (Copy the company name FFD Data into Title)
Vendor Code – Attribute 1 (Copy the vendor code FFD Data into Attribute 1)
Create By – Show the created by email id
Status – Display the external status with next approver and rejection details (when applicable)
Initiation: The user will click on the create option and select the vendor Block /Unblock process.
Form will open with the below details of initiator. Initiator will be able to view only Basic details panel and fields as mentioned below. All the fields will be having common logic with them.
Once the Initiator enters the details and clicks on submit. The system will perform the validation and send the details to the Approver to take action.

approver.description: For India, the workflow will consist of the following approval levels:
Pending with Vertical Head – Review and approval by the relevant business/vertical head.

Pending with MDC Team – Final review by the MDC team. The MDC team can add ad hoc approvers as needed, up to a maximum of five parallel approvers. All ad hoc approvers must record their decisions, after which the request returns to the MDC approver, who then takes the final action in line with the defined common behaviour.
For Non-India, the workflow will consist of four sequential approval levels:
Pending with Country Head – Review and approval by the respective country head.

Pending with MDC Team – Final review by the MDC team. The MDC team can add ad hoc approvers as needed, up to a maximum of five parallel approvers. All ad hoc approvers must record their decisions, after which the request returns to the MDC approver, who then takes the final action in line with the defined common behaviour.

document_elements.content: The primary audience of this document includes business owners for procurement and finance, business analysts, project managers, solution architects, developers, and key end-users involved in vendor onboarding and maintenance. document_elements.content: 2. Project Description

document_elements.content: 2.1 Background

runs.text: 2.1 Background

document_elements.content: Currently, vendor block/Unblock is handled through a mix of manual forms, email-based approvals, and direct entries into the ERP/procurement system, leading to inconsistent data quality, limited visibility, and longer cycle times. The absence of a standardised workflow and controls increases the risk of duplicate vendors, incomplete documentation, and non-compliance with internal and external regulations.

runs.text: Currently, vendor block/Unblock is handled through a mix of manual forms, email-based approvals, and direct entries into the ERP/procurement system, leading to inconsistent data quality, limited visibility, and longer cycle times. The absence of a standardised workflow and controls increases the risk of duplicate vendors, incomplete documentation, and non-compliance with internal and external regulations.

document_elements.content: 2.2 Objectives

runs.text: 2.2 Objectives

document_elements.content: The project aims to design and implement a controlled, workflow-driven vendor block /unblock process that enforces mandatory data fields, documentation checks, and role-based approvals. It also seeks to improve transparency and auditability through clear status tracking, standardized communications, and integration with the organization’s master data and finance systems.

runs.text: The project aims to design and implement a controlled, workflow-driven vendor block /unblock process that enforces mandatory data fields, documentation checks, and role-based approvals. It also seeks to improve transparency and auditability through clear status tracking, standardized communications, and integration with the organization’s master data and finance systems.
